content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-4441

June 5, 1984

Phyllis Matteis
3545 Bell Road
Auburn, CA 95603

Re: Advice Letter No. A-84-146

Dear Mrs. Matteis:

Chairman Stanford asked me to respond to your recent request
for information concerning Senator Ray Johnson's campaign filing
responsibilities.

FACTS

Senator Ray Johnson, a registered Independent, is currently
gathering signatures in an attempt to have his name placed on
the November 6, 1984, ballot. He will not take part in the
primary election which will be held on June 5, 1984. Recently,
you contacted the Placer County Clerk's Office in an effort to
review Senator Johnson's campaign disclosure forms,1/ but the
Clerk was unable to provide you with the particular statements
that you are interested in. In a telephone conversation with
the Clerk's office, I was advised that the last campaign
statements filed with that office by Senator Johnson covered the
period from July 1, 1983, to December 31, 1983.2/ Presumably,
the statements in which you are interested cover the period from
January 1, 1984, to the present.

1/ Senator Johnson is required to file his campaign
statements in Placer County because it is his county of
domicile.

2/ Our records also indicate that these were the last
filings made by Senator Johnson.

Phyllis Matteis
June 5, 1984
Page 2


DISCUSSION

Because Senator Johnson will not be involved in an election
until November 6, 1984, his 1984 campaign filing requirements,
under the Political Reform Act, are as follows:

March and May Threshold Statements:

Senator Johnson was required to file a statement on
March 22, 1984 (covering the period from January 1 - March 17),
and a statement on May 24, 1984 (covering the period from
March 18 - May 19) only if, during the relevant period, his
committee (1) made expenditures of $500 or more, or received or
made contributions of $500 or more, and (2) made a contribution
of $100 or more to, or an independent expenditure of $100 or
more on behalf of, at least one other candidate, controlled
committee, or committee primarily formed to support or oppose a
candidate or ballot measure. If Senator Johnson did not meet
these threshold requirements, he was not required to make any
filings and this may account for the fact that he has not filed
any statements for the period from January 1, 1984, to the
present.

Semi-Annual Statement:

Senator Johnson will be required to file a semi-annual
statement on July 31, 1984, regardless of whether his committee
has any contribution or expenditure activity during the period
from January 31, 1984, to June 30, 1984.

September and October Threshold Statements:

Because Senator Johnson will participate in an election
during the second-half of 1984, he will have to file statements
on September 22, 1984, (covering July 1 - September 17) and
October 25, 1984, (covering September 18 - October 20) if his
committee makes expenditures of $500 or more, or receives or
makes contributions of $500 or more, during the relevant
periods.

Semi-Annual Statement:

Senator Johnson will be required to file a semi-annual
statement on January 31, 1985, regardless of whether his
committee has any contribution or expenditure activity during
the period from July 1 to December 31, 1984.

Phyllis Matteis
June 5, 1984
Page 3


     I hope that this discussion is of help to you.  If you have
any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

                                          Very truly yours,


                                          Janis Shank McLean
                                          Janis Shank McLean
                                          Counsel
                                          Legal Division

JSM:plh

FPFC

MAY 23 9 02 AM '84                           May 24, 1984


State of California
Fair Political Practices Comm.
P.O.Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95804
                                             Attention: Dan Stanford

Gentlemen:

Before me is your Advice letter No. A-84-03C in which you have
told me that Senator Ray Johnson, as a candidate for independent
election to Senate #1, should be filing campaign disclosure state-
ments.

Yesterday, I researched at the Auburn Elections Office and could
not find evidence that he has done this. His residence is in
Roseville and according to your letter, he should file in Placer
County because of this.

As President of Auburn Area Republican Women, Fed. I feel that
the members of my club have the right to find out the source of
Johnson's support in-as-much-as we are all receiving scads of his
campaign literature in the mail.

This is a request for you to investigate this and to take any
action that is proper. Placer County Elections Officers have
said that Johnson need not file because he will not be on a ballot
in June. I say, he is actively campaigning, has opened an office
with a treasurer (indicated on his literature, enclosed) and he
should be compelled to reveal his financial activities.


                                             Sincerely,


                                             Phyllis Matteis
                                             Phyllis Matteis

                                             AUBURN AREA REPUBLICAN WOMEN, FED.
                                             PHYLLIS MATTEIS, PRES.
                                             6545 BELL RD.
                                             AUBURN, CA. 95603

sections.question: Senator Ray Johnson, a registered Independent, is gathering signatures to be placed on the November 6, 1984 ballot and will not participate in the June 5, 1984 primary election. What are his campaign filing requirements under the Political Reform Act for 1984?
sections.conclusion: Because Senator Johnson will not be involved in an election until November 6, 1984, his 1984 campaign filing requirements are: (1) March 22 and May 24 threshold statements only if his committee made expenditures of $500+ or received/made contributions of $500+, and made contributions of $100+ or independent expenditures of $100+ on behalf of at least one other candidate or measure; (2) a semi-annual statement on July 31, 1984, regardless of activity; (3) September 22 and October 25 threshold statements if his committee makes expenditures or contributions of $500+ during those periods; and (4) a semi-annual statement on January 31, 1985, regardless of activity. The absence of filings from January 1, 1984 to present may be explained by failure to meet the threshold requirements.

1/ Senator Johnson is required to file his campaign statements in Placer County because it is his county of domicile.

2/ Our records also indicate that these were the last filings made by Senator Johnson.

Semi-Annual Statement:

Phyllis Matteis  
June 5, 1984  

I hope that this discussion is of help to you. If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure filing requirements for an independent candidate who is gathering signatures for a November ballot but not participating in the June primary election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: An independent candidate gathering signatures for a November ballot must file semi-annual statements on July 31, 1984 and January 31, 1985 regardless of activity, and may need to file threshold statements in March, May, September, and October only if specific contribution and expenditure thresholds are met.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated May 24, 1984) and the FPPC response letter (dated June 5, 1984). The response provides detailed discussion of filing requirements organized by filing period and threshold conditions. High confidence in extraction; minor uncertainty in question verbatim selection due to distributed information across Facts and Discussion sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Senator Ray Johnson, a registered Independent, is gathering signatures to be placed on the November 6, 1984 ballot and will not participate in the June 5, 1984 primary election. What are his campaign filing requirements under the Political Reform Act for 1984?

CONCLUSION: Because Senator Johnson will not be involved in an election until November 6, 1984, his 1984 campaign filing requirements are: (1) March 22 and May 24 threshold statements only if his committee made expenditures of $500+ or received/made contributions of $500+, and made contributions of $100+ or independent expenditures of $100+ on behalf of at least one other candidate or measure; (2) a semi-annual statement on July 31, 1984, regardless of activity; (3) September 22 and October 25 threshold statements if his committee makes expenditures or contributions of $500+ during those periods; and (4) a semi-annual statement on January 31, 1985, regardless of activity. The absence of filings from January 1, 1984 to present may be explained by failure to meet the threshold requirements.

embedding.summary: FPPC counsel responds to an inquiry about Senator Ray Johnson's campaign disclosure filing obligations as an independent candidate gathering signatures for the November 1984 ballot, explaining his filing schedule and threshold requirements throughout 2024.
